summary: Donkey Kong cleanup/refactor with explicitly stated "no match% increase". The main change split generic walk helper code out of Donkey-specific naming: ftdonkey3.c became src/melee/ft/ftwalkcommon.c, ftdonkey2.c became ftdonkey_walk.c, and callers in C and remaining asm labels were renamed from ftDonkey_* walk helpers to ftWalkCommon_*. The PR also reorganized ftDonkeyAttributes with named nested groups for Cargo Hold and special moves, updated DK code to use those field names, added ftwalkcommon.h prototypes, and clarified a small common fighter helper name/prototype around scaleBy154_8008D8D8 and func_8008D8E8.
what_changed: Renamed Donkey walk helper symbols to common fighter walk symbols: ftDonkey_GetWalkType_800DFBF8, ftDonkey_800DFC70, ftDonkey_800DFCA4, ftDonkey_800DFDDC, ftDonkey_800DFEC8, and ftDonkey_800E0060 became ftWalkCommon_* equivalents.
Moved the generic walk implementation from src/melee/ft/chara/ftDonkey/ftdonkey3.c to src/melee/ft/ftwalkcommon.c and added src/melee/ft/ftwalkcommon.h with prototypes for the common walk helpers.

Renamed src/melee/ft/chara/ftDonkey/ftdonkey2.c to src/melee/ft/chara/ftDonkey/ftdonkey_walk.c and adjusted it to call the new ftWalkCommon_* helpers while keeping DK-specific wrappers such as ftDonkey_800DFB34, ftDonkey_800DFB54, and ftDonkey_800DFBB8.
Updated asm call labels in asm/melee/ft/chara/ftkirby.s and asm/melee/ft/code_800877F8.s so existing assembly references point at ftWalkCommon_* instead of old ftDonkey_* names.
Updated obj_files.mk linker object list to replace ftdonkey2.c.o and ftdonkey3.c.o with ftdonkey_walk.c.o and ftwalkcommon.c.o while preserving linker order.
Expanded ftDonkeyAttributes from mostly x-offset fields into named groups including action_state, cargo_hold, SpecialN, SpecialS, SpecialHi, and SpecialLw-related fields.

Updated DK source files to use the new ftDonkeyAttributes names, for example action_state instead of x0, cargo_hold.x20_TURN_SPEED instead of x20, cargo_hold.x24_JUMP_STARTUP_LAG instead of x24, cargo_hold.x28_LANDING_LAG instead of x28, and SpecialHi velocity/gravity/landing-lag fields instead of raw x4C/x50/x54/x58/x5C/x60/x64 offsets.
Renamed asm symbol func_8008D8D8 to scaleBy154_8008D8D8 and changed include/functions.h to expose f32 scaleBy154_8008D8D8(f32) and s32 func_8008D8E8(f32).
Changed ftDonkey_800E0750 to pass scaleBy154_8008D8D8(fighter->dmg.x1850_forceApplied) into func_8008D8E8 instead of calling func_8008D8D8 separately and then func_8008D8E8 with no visible argument.
Added a parameter name, damageAmount, to the func_8007ABD0 prototype in include/functions.h.
smart_moves: Recognized that walk behavior previously living under ftDonkey_* names was generic enough to belong in ftWalkCommon, which improves reuse for other fighters and makes asm references less misleading.
Kept function addresses in symbol names while improving semantic prefixes, a common decomp compromise that preserves searchability and matching context.
Propagated symbol renames through both C and asm callsites, avoiding split-brain naming where decompiled C and leftover assembly disagree about the same routine.
Added a dedicated ftwalkcommon.h instead of continuing to export common walking helpers through the Donkey header.
Grouped ftDonkeyAttributes fields by move family, which gives future special-move cleanup a safer map from raw offsets to intended gameplay parameters.
Preserved linker-order awareness in obj_files.mk when moving files, which is important for a decomp project even when the PR does not increase match percentage.
decomp_lessons: When a character file contains behavior that is called from common fighter code or other character asm, consider whether the symbol belongs under a common module prefix rather than the character prefix.
A cleanup PR can be valuable even with no match percentage increase if it reduces misleading names and isolates common logic for later matching work.
File renames should be paired with obj_files.mk updates that keep the original link position unless there is evidence the linker order should change.
For struct cleanup, prefer named nested groups that reflect move families while retaining offset-like field names inside them when full semantics are not yet proven.

If a helper is identified by a constant offset, a descriptive interim name like scaleBy154_8008D8D8 is better than a bare func_* name, but the address suffix remains useful until a final name is known.
Update remaining assembly labels after C-side symbol renames; otherwise future researchers searching for a symbol will miss active references.
naming_conventions: Generic fighter walk helpers use the ftWalkCommon_ prefix rather than ftDonkey_ when not DK-specific.
Renamed file ftdonkey2.c to ftdonkey_walk.c to describe the DK-specific walk wrapper role.
Renamed common walk implementation file from a DK-numbered file to ftwalkcommon.c, with matching header ftwalkcommon.h.
ftDonkeyAttributes uses grouped move names such as cargo_hold, SpecialN, SpecialS, SpecialHi, and SpecialLw.
New attribute fields combine original offset hints with semantic names, for example x20_TURN_SPEED, x24_JUMP_STARTUP_LAG, x28_LANDING_LAG, x4C_AERIAL_VERTICAL_VELOCITY, and x64_LANDING_LAG.
The helper at 8008D8D8 was renamed scaleBy154_8008D8D8, reflecting that it multiplies by p_ftCommonData offset 0x154.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Renamed global assembly label func_8008D8D8 to scaleBy154_8008D8D8 without changing the instruction body shown in the diff.
Changed branch-and-link annotations in leftover asm from ftDonkey_* to ftWalkCommon_* while preserving the same encoded call instructions/addresses in the diff excerpt.
Used C prototypes in include/functions.h to clarify calls to assembly-backed helpers, including scaleBy154_8008D8D8(f32) and func_8008D8E8(f32).
Maintained object-file link order while swapping renamed source objects into obj_files.mk.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body explicitly says: "Dk cleanup - no match% increase."
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that build on ftWalkCommon_* or rename these helpers again.
Search for remaining ftDonkey_* walk helper references after PR 442 to verify rename completeness.
Inspect later ftDonkeyAttributes cleanup to see whether grouped fields got final non-offset names.
Search for scaleBy154_8008D8D8 and func_8008D8E8 usage to confirm the helper signature and semantics.
Compare object link order around ftdonkey_walk.c.o and ftwalkcommon.c.o in later obj_files.mk revisions.
